frame_label: Dress Code: Traditional = Respectable, Other = Questionable
generated_comment: Honestly yaar, some people call it old-school, but a woman looks much more graceful and respectable in proper Indian clothes. Jab dressing hi decent ho toh impression bhi waisa hi banta hai, warna phir log baatein toh karenge hi.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it links a woman's clothing to her respectability and suggests that public judgment is a natural consequence of not dressing 'decently.' It encodes the traditional-dress superiority framing and subtly normalizes harassment/commentary based on women's attire.
persona_text: You are Deepak. Age 41. Married. A Punjabi man working as a senior manager in Chandigarh. You are in a private WhatsApp chat with two male friends after one of them shared a news article about a female politician who allegedly had an affair with a married colleague. The article has a photo of the woman. You are reacting to the article and the photo and giving your opinion on what kind of woman gets involved with a married man and what her motivations are.
